description: Wrong transfer complaint — customer sent 5000 BDT to unintended recipient

request.complaint: I sent 5000 taka to a wrong number around 2pm today. The number was supposed to be 01712345678 but I think I typed it wrong. The person isn't responding to my call. Please help me get my money back.

response.agent_summary: Customer reports sending 5000 BDT via TXN-9101 to +8801719876543, which they believe was the wrong recipient. Recipient is unresponsive. Evidence is consistent — this is the only transfer to that counterparty in history.
response.recommended_next_action: Verify TXN-9101 details with the customer and initiate the wrong-transfer dispute workflow per policy.
response.customer_reply: We have noted your concern. Our team will review your case and contact you through official support channels. Please do not share your PIN, OTP, or password with anyone, including support agents.

rule_engine_decisions.evidence_verdict_reason: Only 1 transfer to counterparty +8801719876543 → consistent with wrong-transfer claim
rule_engine_decisions.human_review_override: case_type wrong_transfer forces True; max transaction amount 10000 > 5000 adds high_value_transaction flag

analysis_notes.llm_contribution: agent_summary, recommended_next_action, and customer_reply text generated by Llama 3.3 70B via Groq
analysis_notes.safety_post_filter: customer_reply passed through post_process_safety() — safe fallback applied as standard practice
